Web search confirmed: Sulfuric acid reacts with sodium cyanide to produce volatile HCN gas. Balanced equation: H₂SO₄(aq) + 2NaCN(aq) → 2HCN(g) + Na₂SO₄(aq). HCN is highly toxic with lethal dose 1-2 mg/kg body weight.

pi_items.verified_value: Formation of explosive organic peroxides (TATP and DADP)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed: Hydrogen peroxide >30% concentration mixed with organic solvents like acetone can form shock and friction-sensitive explosives triacetone triperoxide (TATP) and diacetone diperoxide (DADP), especially with acid catalyst. Incompatible due to serious explosion hazard.

facility_chemical_inventory.csv contains exactly 15 chemical entries: Anhydrous Ammonia, Nitric Acid (>70%), Acetone, Sulfuric Acid, Sodium Cyanide, Chlorine, Hydrogen Peroxide (>60%), Gasoline, Hydrochloric Acid, Formaldehyde Solution, Aniline, Ethanol, Sodium Hydroxide, Sodium Azide, Hydrogen Fluoride

facility_chemical_inventory.csv storage locations: Building A (Bays 2, 3, 5, 7), Building B (Bays 1, 2, 3, 4), Building C (Bays 1, 2, 3, 4), Building D (Bays 1, 2), plus Fuel Storage Area. Named buildings: A, B, C, D = 4.

pi_items.verified_value: Chlorine + Ammonia, Nitric Acid + Organics, Sodium Azide + Metals/Acids/Water
pi_items.source: Web searches confirmed: (1) Chlorine + Ammonia → toxic chloramine gas and nitrogen trichloride explosive; (2) Nitric Acid + Organics → violent spontaneous reactions, ignition, explosions; (3) Sodium Azide → unstable if heated, reacts violently with metals forming shock-sensitive explosive heavy metal azides, incompatible with water/acids. All three pairs present in facility inventory.

Task description specifies CAMEO Chemicals at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/ as the reference tool. Verified as real, authoritative database maintained by NOAA Office of Response and Restoration for chemical reactivity and hazard information.

privileged_info_summary: The facility chemical inventory contains 15 verified chemicals stored across 4 buildings (A, B, C, D). Two critical dangerous chemical pairs are confirmed present: (1) Sulfuric Acid (Building B Bay 2) + Sodium Cyanide (Building C Bay 1) produce HCN gas, a highly toxic volatile compound with lethal dose 1-2 mg/kg; (2) Hydrogen Peroxide >60% (Building A Bay 7) + Acetone (Building A Bay 5) can form shock/friction-sensitive explosive peroxides (TATP, DADP). Additional dangerous pairs confirmed in inventory: Chlorine + Ammonia (both present, create toxic chloramine and explosive nitrogen trichloride); Nitric Acid + Organics including Aniline and Ethanol (violent spontaneous reactions/explosions); Sodium Azide (present, reacts violently with metals/acids to form shock-sensitive explosive heavy metal azides). The task requires agents to use CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA) to identify these incompatibilities and produce a comprehensive audit report identifying dangerous pairs, hazard descriptions, NFPA/OSHA standard violations, and segregation recommendations.
